MSFT.reasoning: Macro regime: Transitional/cautious. Fed held at 3.5-3.75% (third consecutive hold, higher-for-longer confirmed), US-Iran military conflict driving oil spike and 4.4% IMF inflation forecast, and Meta's -9% capex shock creates contagion risk for AI cloud spending multiples. The AI tailwind (Alphabet/Amazon beat) is intact but the macro backdrop is not clearly risk-on. Relative strength: MSFT's 5d ROC of -1.92% and 10d ROC of -2.97% indicate recent underperformance vs the S&P +9% April run. Price is now below the Apr 17 prior entry ($420.26) and below all short-term EMAs (EMA-5 at $419.01, EMA-10 at $417.11). Price sits barely above EMA-50 ($406.60, +0.29%) — a thin margin. The April 30 session logged 70.8M volume (2.06x 20-day avg) on a down day from $424.46 to $407.78 — a high-conviction distribution signal. Daily OBV trend is down. Asymmetry math fails the 2:1 minimum bar: entry near $410, upside to nearest resistance $413.05 = ~0.75% or to recent high $433.70 = ~5.8%, downside to nearest support $381.71 = ~7.1%. Even to the further target, R/R is approximately 5.8% upside vs 7.1% downside = 0.82:1, well below the 2:1 minimum. Hourly picture: hourly RSI-21 at 39.01 approaching oversold, hourly OBV trending up, and hourly support at $411.41 (5 tests) has held — these suggest a near-term bounce is possible but does not fix the unfavorable daily-timeframe R/R. Capital preservation: MSFT balance sheet is pristine — no leverage flag. Sector cycle timing: the 21d ROC is +10.16%, suggesting we are later in the current leg, not early. Wiki thesis notes prior bullish calls were confirmed at lower prices; current price is below the last recorded buy entry, indicating the prior technical thesis has partially unwound. Regime continuity: wiki macro regime labeled 'mixed-to-cautious' as of 2026-04-30 — today's data is consistent, no regime change to signal. Pass: asymmetry is 0.82:1, macro is not decisively risk-on, and recent high-volume distribution day breaks the bullish thesis.
